pages.text: $(o)"The three were once one — until each chose a different truth."$()$(br2)The $(l)Arena$() is the ancient proving-plane, holding the eternal balance of three factions: $(l)Light, Void, Transcendence$().

pages.title: Light Faction
pages.text: The $(l)Elemental Warden$() commands the "outer" aspects of the eighteen elements — fire, ice, lightning, wind, earth, nature, holy, light.$(br2)He guards $(l)visible power$(). His combat favors rhythm and elemental rotation — the most familiar opponent, and the easiest to grow proud against.

pages.title: Void Faction
pages.text: The $(l)Void Weaver$() masters the "inner" aspects — dark, void, space, time, sonic.$(br2)She does not fight head-on; she $(l)rewrites the battlefield$(). Her offense is "what you do wrong, amplified".$(br2)$(o)"What light cannot reach is the bone of the world."$() — Weaver's epigraph

pages.title: Transcendence Faction
pages.text: The $(l)Transcendence Avatar$() is the "nineteenth thread" beyond the eighteen elements — Transcendence itself.$(br2)Defeating her is not victory; it is $(l)acknowledgment$(). Records name only seven mages who beat the Avatar — three went mute on the spot, never casting again.

pages.title: Triangle Balance
pages.text: $(l)Same-faction units ally; different factions feud$() — strictly enforced.$(br2)Aggro two factions and you face doubled hostility; ally with one and unexpected aid follows.$(br2)$(o)"Enemy of enemy is not necessarily friend — but enemy of enemy's enemy always is."$()
